source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Retention Policies on Cloud Storage Buckets Used for Exporting Logs Are Configured Using Bucket Lock

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Retention Policies on Cloud Storage Buckets Used for Exporting Logs Are Configured Using Bucket Lock
input_row.description: Enabling retention policies on log buckets will protect logs stored in cloud storage buckets from being overwritten or accidentally deleted. It is recommended to set up retention policies and configure Bucket Lock on all storage buckets that are used as log sinks.
input_row.rationale: Logs can be exported by creating one or more sinks that include a log filter and a destination. As Cloud Logging receives new log entries, they are compared against each sink. If a log entry matches a sink's filter, then a copy of the log entry is written to the destination. Sinks can be configured to export logs in storage buckets. It is recommended to configure a data retention policy for these cloud storage buckets and to lock the data retention policy; thus permanently preventing the policy from being reduced or removed. This way, if the system is ever compromised by an attacker or a malicious insider who wants to cover their tracks, the activity logs are definitely preserved for forensics and security investigations. Impact: Locking a bucket is an irreversible action. Once you lock a bucket, you cannot remove the retention policy from the bucket or decrease the retention period for the policy. You will then have to wait for the retention period for all items within the bucket before you can delete them, and then the bucket.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Open the Cloud Storage browser in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser. 2. In the Column display options menu, make sure Retention policy is checked. 3. In the list of buckets, the retention period of each bucket is found in the Retention policy column. If the retention policy is locked, an image of a lock appears directly to the left of the retention period. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To list all sinks destined to storage buckets: gcloud logging sinks list --folder=FOLDER_ID | --organization=ORGANIZATION_ID | --project=PROJECT_ID 2. For every storage bucket listed above, verify that retention policies and Bucket Lock are enabled: gsutil retention get gs://BUCKET_NAME For more information, see https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/using-bucket- lock#view-policy.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. If sinks are not configured, first follow the instructions in the recommendation: Ensure that sinks are configured for all Log entries. 2. For each storage bucket configured as a sink, go to the Cloud Storage browser at https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser/<BUCKET_NAME>. 3. Select the Bucket Lock tab near the top of the page. 4. In the Retention policy entry, click the Add Duration link. The Set a retention policy dialog box appears. 5. Enter the desired length of time for the retention period and click Save policy. 6. Set the Lock status for this retention policy to Locked. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To list all sinks destined to storage buckets: gcloud logging sinks list --folder=FOLDER_ID | --organization=ORGANIZATION_ID | --project=PROJECT_ID 2. For each storage bucket listed above, set a retention policy and lock it: gsutil retention set [TIME_DURATION] gs://[BUCKET_NAME] gsutil retention lock gs://[BUCKET_NAME] For more information, visit https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/using-bucket-lock#set- policy. Default Value: By default, storage buckets used as log sinks do not have retention policies and Bucket Lock configured. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/bucket-lock 2. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/using-bucket-lock 3. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/bucket-lock Additional Information: Caution: Locking a retention policy is an irreversible action. Once locked, you must delete the entire bucket in order to "remove" the bucket's retention policy. However, before you can delete the bucket, you must be able to delete all the objects in the bucket, which itself is only possible if all the objects have reached the retention period set by the retention policy.
input_row.impact: Locking a bucket is an irreversible action. Once you lock a bucket, you cannot remove the retention policy from the bucket or decrease the retention period for the policy. You will then have to wait for the retention period for all items within the bucket before you can delete them, and then the bucket.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/bucket-lock 2. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/using-bucket-lock 3. https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/bucket-lock Additional Information: Caution: Locking a retention policy is an irreversible action. Once locked, you must delete the entire bucket in order to "remove" the bucket's retention policy. However, before you can delete the bucket, you must be able to delete all the objects in the bucket, which itself is only possible if all the objects have reached the retention period set by the retention policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be fully verified using GCP APIs and CLI commands. The Audit section provides all necessary Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and gsutil commands needed to verify if retention policies and Bucket Lock are enabled on storage buckets used for exporting logs. These capabilities allow compliance checks to be automated without requiring manual intervention.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_storage_bucket_retention_policy_locked

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP API/CLI Commands**:
  1. Use the `gcloud logging sinks list` command to identify all log sinks destined for storage buckets. This allows you to list all relevant storage buckets.
     ```bash
     gcloud logging sinks list --project=PROJECT_ID
     ```
  2. For each bucket identified, use the `gsutil retention get` command to verify the retention policy and check if it is locked.
     ```bash
     gsutil retention get gs://BUCKET_NAME
     ```
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate**:
  - Enumerate all storage buckets used as log sinks within the specified project/folder/organization.
  
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate**:
  - Confirm that each storage bucket has a retention policy configured.
  - Ensure the retention policy is locked, indicated by a lock icon in CLI output or via a field in the console.
  
- **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
  - **Pass**: A retention policy is set and is marked as "locked" for each storage bucket used as a log sink.
  - **Fail**: Either no retention policy is set, or it is not locked for any of the considered buckets.

This approach leverages GCP's tooling to comprehensively audit compliance and ensures a consistent check across multiple buckets efficiently.
step2_review:
---
### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. Both the identification of log sinks and the verification of retention policy/lock status on buckets can be performed using GCP APIs and CLI tools.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Feasibility:**  
  - `gcloud logging sinks list` reliably enumerates log sinks targeting Cloud Storage buckets.
  - `gsutil retention get` (and also `gsutil retention lock` for enforcement) can programmatically check retention policy status and lock state.
- **Other GCP Capabilities:**  
  - GCP Config (now called Config Controller) does not natively monitor bucket retention/lock, so CLI/API is the correct approach.
  - CloudFormation is AWS-specific and not relevant here.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Strengths:**
  - Correct use of `gcloud logging sinks list` to enumerate log sinks.
  - Correct use of `gsutil retention get` to check retention policy and lock status.
  - Pass/fail criteria are clear and map to control requirements.

- **Gaps/Inaccuracies:**
  - **Program Name:**  
    The program name `gcp_storage_bucket_retention_policy_locked` is descriptive but could be more explicit, e.g., `gcp_storage_bucket_log_sink_retention_policy_locked` to clarify that only log sink buckets are in scope.
  - **Retention Lock Validation:**  
    The description mentions "lock icon in CLI output or via a field in the console." For automation, the CLI output should be parsed for `"isLocked": true` (JSON output) or equivalent, not visual icons.
  - **API Alternative:**  
    The assessment does not mention the GCP REST API (`storage.buckets.get`) which can also be used to programmatically check retention policy and lock status for full automation.
  - **Edge Cases:**  
    The assessment does not explicitly mention handling buckets in other projects (cross-project sinks) or organization-level sinks.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Program Name:**  
  Use `gcp_storage_bucket_log_sink_retention_policy_locked` for clarity and alignment with naming conventions.
- **Automation Details:**
  - Explicitly state that the output of `gsutil retention get` should be parsed for `"isLocked": true` (for scripting/automation).
  - Consider using `gsutil retention get -j` for JSON output, which is easier to parse programmatically.
  - Mention the use of the Storage API (`storage.buckets.get`) as an alternative for organizations that prefer API calls over CLI.
- **Scope Clarification:**
  - Clarify how to handle log sinks that point to buckets in other projects or organizations.
  - Ensure that all log sinks (including organization- and folder-level sinks) are enumerated, not just project-level.
- **Additional Security Best Practice:**
  - Recommend checking that retention periods meet organizational policy (e.g., minimum days/months), not just that a policy is set and locked.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The automation approach is fundamentally sound and leverages the correct GCP tools.
- Minor improvements are needed for clarity and completeness, but the technical direction is correct.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

**Required Modifications:**
- Update the program name to `gcp_storage_bucket_log_sink_retention_policy_locked`.
- Specify that the automation should parse `"isLocked": true` from the CLI/JSON output, not rely on visual cues.
- Optionally, reference the GCP Storage API as an alternative method.
- Clarify enumeration of all possible log sinks (project, folder, organization) and handling of cross-project buckets.
- (Optional) Add a check for minimum retention period if required by policy.

---

**Summary Table**

| Section             | Assessment         | Notes                                                                                   |
|---------------------|-------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE              | Correctly classified as Automated; feasible with GCP CLI/APIs.                          |
| Technical Accuracy  | 8/10               | Mostly correct; minor improvements needed for naming, output parsing, and completeness.  |
| Improvements        | See above          | Naming, output parsing, API alternative, scope clarification.                           |
| Confidence          | HIGH               | Approach is fundamentally correct.                                                      |
| Final Recommendation| MODIFY             | Update naming, clarify parsing, expand on scope and API alternatives.                   |
